ranking.reason: 极具技术深度且解决了Agent开发中的实际工程痛点，非常适合科研/工程类受众。
宏观趋势分析，为科研人员提供职业发展和技术选型的参考。
及时的模型更新信息，适合作为科研工具箱的补充。

ideas.workingTitle: 告别Agent报错！从BabyAGI 4看Agent架构的未来
ideas.angle: 通过对比传统Agent与Active Graph架构，解析如何通过“事件日志”实现Agent的断点续传与状态管理。
ideas.whyNow: Agent开发正从实验阶段走向工程化，稳定性是目前最大的瓶颈。

ideas.workingTitle: 2026 AI工程现状：为什么你的Agent还停留在“读写”阶段？
ideas.angle: 结合行业报告，分析Agent从“阅读总结”到“自主行动”的演进，以及科研人员必须掌握的工程约束（成本与评估）。
ideas.whyNow: 年中是回顾技术趋势、调整科研方向的最佳时机。

ideas.workingTitle: 科研人的模型选型手册：Gemini 3.6 Flash系列怎么选？
ideas.angle: 对比Gemini 3.6 Flash与Lite/Cyber版本的差异，给出不同科研场景下的最佳实践建议。
ideas.whyNow: 模型更新频繁，科研人员需要快速筛选出最适合自己任务的工具。

items.author: AI Engineer
items.subtitle: YouTube
items.title: Active Graph Agent Runtime (BabyAGI 4) — Yohei Nakajima, Untapped Capital
items.excerpt: Yohei Nakajima was running a 500 question eval when his API key died at question 350. Normally that means restarting the whole long agent from scratch. Instead it rolled back one step and resumed at 353, because in ActiveGraph the log is the agent. Most people build agents around the LLM and bolt on memory and logging; Nakajima, the creator of BabyAGI, flips it and builds around an immutable event log. Every action and every change to the agent flattens into one typed log, which projects a graph that is the agent's state, so you get replays, rollbacks, and forks for free. On top of the log sit behaviors that react to graph changes and emit events, policies that decide what the agent may change on its own versus what needs a human or a contradiction check, and swappable packs for memory, tools, and chat. The LLMs never talk to each other; they only touch shared state, an idea he borrows from 1970s blackboard systems and Kafka, and his hunch is that AI writes this style better than modern agent code because it has decades of training data on it. The payoff is self improvement: a loop that forks the agent, proposes a patch, gates it behind sandbox tests, and keeps it only if accuracy actually rises, and a lab that reads his blog posts, runs its own experiments, and once found a bug in its own code and opened the PR. Speaker info: - https://x.com/yoheinakajima - https://www.linkedin.com/in/yoheinakajima - https://yohei.me Timestamps: 0:00 - ActiveGraph and three years of BabyAGI 1:55 - Build around the log, not the LLM 3:24 - Behaviors, policies, and views 6:43 - Packs and the blackboard architecture lineage 8:12 - The log as memory, and the API key that resumed itself 9:53 - Reference agents built natively on the log 11:11 - Self improvement: regimes and controlled self m…
items.summary: BabyAGI创始人Yohei Nakajima提出了一种基于“不可变事件日志”的Active Graph Agent架构，通过将日志作为Agent状态，实现了自动回滚、重放和分叉功能。

editorial.summaryZh: BabyAGI创始人Yohei Nakajima提出了一种基于“不可变事件日志”的Active Graph Agent架构，通过将日志作为Agent状态，实现了自动回滚、重放和分叉功能。
editorial.whyItMattersZh: 这是Agent开发范式的重大转变：从“以LLM为中心”转向“以状态日志为中心”，解决了Agent在长任务中因API中断而导致任务失败的痛点。
editorial.evidenceZh: 通过将所有动作扁平化为类型化日志，Agent可以在API中断后从中断点（如第350题）自动恢复，无需从头开始。
editorial.uncertaintyZh: 需核实该架构在复杂生产环境下的性能开销，建议回到原始视频核实其具体实现细节。
editorial.chineseAudienceGapZh: 国内Agent开发多关注Prompt工程或简单的LangChain调用，缺乏对底层架构（如Blackboard系统、事件溯源）的深度讨论。
editorial.rednoteAngleZh: 用“Agent总是跑一半报错？”作为痛点，对比传统Agent与Active Graph架构的差异，展示如何通过日志实现“断点续传”。

items.author: AI Engineer
items.subtitle: YouTube
items.title: 2026 State of AI Engineering — Barr Yaron, Amplify Partners
items.excerpt: Barr Yaron shares her perspective on the results and emerging state of AI engineering in 2026. Speaker: Barr Yaron — Partner, Amplify Partners Barr backs founders building the AI infrastructure and applications that will shape the future. Links: X: https://x.com/barrnanas LinkedIn: https://linkedin.com/in/barryaron Website: https://barrchives.com Timestamps 0:00 Introduction and Survey Context 2:26 The AI Engineering Workforce 3:21 Current Modalities and Adoption 5:34 Model Strategy: Closed vs. Open-Weight 8:20 Cost as an Engineering Constraint 9:36 The Rise of Agentic Workflows 11:57 Infrastructure Challenges and Evals 12:53 The Build vs. Buy Trade-off 14:09 Impact on Engineering Culture and Teams 16:27 Future Bets and Predictions Quotes: "The median new engineer has nearly as much AI experience as the median 10-year software veteran." (3:07) "Cost is now a first-class engineering constraint." (8:38) "Agents are no longer reading, summarizing, drafting—they’re taking actions inside of systems." (10:58) "The biggest challenge in your stack? Every single year, the number one answer is eval." (12:12) "Shipping software is not gated on being an engineer anymore." (16:17)
items.summary: Amplify Partners发布的《2026 AI工程现状报告》指出，AI工程已进入Agentic Workflow时代，且成本已成为工程决策的首要约束。

editorial.summaryZh: Amplify Partners发布的《2026 AI工程现状报告》指出，AI工程已进入Agentic Workflow时代，且成本已成为工程决策的首要约束。
editorial.whyItMattersZh: 提供了行业宏观视角，明确了AI工程从简单的模型调用转向复杂的Agent协作，以及对工程文化的影响。
editorial.evidenceZh: 报告指出Agent已不再仅限于阅读和总结，而是开始在系统内部采取实际行动；同时强调了成本作为一级工程约束的重要性。
editorial.uncertaintyZh: 该内容为curated来源，建议回到原始视频核实报告中关于“Build vs Buy”的具体数据支撑。
editorial.chineseAudienceGapZh: 国内对AI工程化的讨论多停留在模型能力，缺乏对工程文化、成本约束和Agent工作流演进的系统性总结。
editorial.rednoteAngleZh: 制作一张“2026 AI工程趋势图”，对比2024与2026年的工程重点变化，帮助科研人员调整技术栈方向。

items.author: Google DeepMind
items.subtitle: Google DeepMind
items.title: Introducing Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and 3.5 Flash Cyber
items.excerpt: We’re introducing new Gemini models, including Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite and 3.5 Flash Cyber.
items.summary: Google发布Gemini 3.6 Flash及3.5 Flash-Lite/Cyber系列模型，进一步细分了轻量化与垂直领域模型矩阵。

editorial.summaryZh: Google发布Gemini 3.6 Flash及3.5 Flash-Lite/Cyber系列模型，进一步细分了轻量化与垂直领域模型矩阵。
editorial.whyItMattersZh: 模型矩阵的细分意味着科研人员在选择基座模型时有了更精准的成本与性能权衡空间。
editorial.evidenceZh: 明确提到了Gemini 3.6 Flash以及针对特定场景的Flash-Lite和Cyber版本。
editorial.chineseAudienceGapZh: 缺乏对这些新模型在科研任务（如代码生成、长文本处理）中的具体评测对比。
editorial.rednoteAngleZh: 整理一份“Gemini模型选型指南”，根据不同科研场景（如轻量化部署、网络安全分析）推荐对应的Flash版本。
